message:
chore: close bead ft-3681t.4.6 (robot idempotency/dedup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2794,7 +2794,7 @@
 {"id": "ft-3681t.4.3", "title": "[robot] Transactional action engine (dry-run, approval, rollback)", "description": "Build transaction-like action semantics for high-impact operations: dry-run previews, policy checks, approval checkpoints, execution receipts, and rollback strategies when possible.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. High-impact commands support dry-run and approval gates. 2. Execution receipts include enough detail for audit and debugging. 3. Rollback/compensation behavior is explicit.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "in_progress", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "PinkSpire", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:12:48.134973Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-08T00:05:35.791456Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["robot-api", "safety", "transactions"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.3", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:12:48.134973Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.3", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:16:58.893063Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.3", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:16:59.365103Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1998, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.3", "author": "PinkSpire", "text": "Implemented tx-engine semantics in crates/frankenterm-core/src/plan.rs plus CLI/MCP parity wiring: commit now validates lifecycle state, emits deterministic per-step receipts, records failure_boundary, blocks via explicit KillSwitchBlocked/PauseSuspended outcomes instead of hard errors, and only auto-compensates when there are committed steps to unwind. Compensation now validates lifecycle state, runs in reverse commit order, emits receipts, and reports compensated/failed/skipped counts. Updated tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh assertions for explicit commit/rollback counts/receipts and added paused + safe-mode scenarios. Verification via rch with isolated caches: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib tx_ -- --nocapture passed (145 passed, includes new plan.rs tests); cargo test -p frankenterm mission_robot_tx_helpers_build_expected_inputs_and_reports -- --nocapture passed. Deterministic e2e script currently fails before exercising tx logic because remote rch cargo run cannot resolve sibling path dependency /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config on the worker; artifacts in tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260307_185929.*.", "created_at": "2026-03-08T00:05:35Z"}]}
 {"id": "ft-3681t.4.4", "title": "[robot] Machine contracts, SDK generation, ntm-compat shim, replay tests", "description": "Publish durable machine contracts (schemas/specs/examples), generate client SDK surfaces, add an ntm compatibility shim for migration acceleration, and enforce behavior via replay-based contract tests.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Contracts and generated clients stay in lockstep with implementation. 2. Compatibility shim supports migration-critical workflows. 3. Replay tests catch protocol regressions.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:12:54.039676Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-02T19:31:27.907853Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["contracts", "migration", "robot-api"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.4", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:12:54.039676Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.4", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:16:59.839104Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.4", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:00.324144Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.4", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:00.801484Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "ft-3681t.4.5", "title": "[quality] Robot API contract/replay and compatibility test matrix", "description": "Build a contract matrix that validates schema stability, deterministic semantics, replay correctness, and ntm-compat migration guarantees for machine interfaces.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Contract tests cover every robot command/event/error surface. 2. Replay suite verifies determinism and backward-compat migration expectations. 3. Compatibility failures are pinpointed with actionable diffs. 4. Includes unit/integration/e2e contract tests with detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:24:03.137786Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:28.839160Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["contracts", "quality-gate", "robot-api"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:24:03.137786Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:25:43.483212Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:25:44.413864Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:25:46.040695Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:25:47.875759Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.4.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.4.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:35:28.839105Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
-{"id": "ft-3681t.4.6", "title": "[robot] Idempotency keys, dedupe, and at-least-once safe mutation semantics", "description": "Add idempotency and deduplication semantics for mutating robot actions to make retries safe under network failures, reconnects, and orchestrator restarts.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Mutating robot APIs support idempotency keys and deterministic replay-safe outcomes. 2. Duplicate request handling is explicit and observable. 3. Failure/retry semantics prevent unintended repeated side effects. Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic. Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario. Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code. Q5. Robot-serializable types in robot_types.rs.", "close_reason": "Implemented in 20c443a0: MutationKey, MutationGuard, MutationOutcome with TTL eviction, FIFO capacity, batch checks, 35+ tests"}
 {"id": "ft-3681t.5", "title": "[epic] Flywheel connector fabric as native FrankenTerm subsystem", "description": "# Epic 5: Flywheel Connector Fabric Integration

Integrate flywheel connector system components as a first-class FrankenTerm subsystem: host runtime, registry, sandboxing, lifecycle, event bridges, mesh federation, and audit chain.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Connectors are installable/runnable under policy and capability constraints.
2. Bi-directional event flow between FrankenTerm and connectors is reliable.
3. Multi-host connector federation is operationally viable.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "open", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:44.148717Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-02T19:30:46.568150Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["connectors", "epic", "fcp-integration"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:44.148717Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:49.741297Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.5", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:09:50.159223Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1722, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.5", "author": "Jeff Emanuel", "text": "Design note: FCP integration is deep-by-design. We are not adding one-off connectors; we are bringing host/runtime/security/mesh/audit primitives into FrankenTerm so external system automation is governed by the same policy and telemetry contracts as terminal actions.", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:56Z"}]}
 {"id": "ft-3681t.5.1", "title": "[connectors] Embed FCP host/protocol runtime into FrankenTerm", "description": "Integrate core flywheel connector runtime components (fcp-core, fcp-protocol, fcp-host) as native FrankenTerm services with explicit host lifecycle control, resource-isolation budgets, and deterministic failure containment rather than external bolt-ons.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. Connector host runtime is runnable and observable inside FrankenTerm with explicit health/liveness signals.
2. Protocol boundaries between control plane and connectors are explicit, versioned, and testable.
3. Failure isolation and resource budgeting (CPU/memory/IO limits) are enforced to protect terminal responsiveness.
4. Host lifecycle operations (start/stop/restart/upgrade) are deterministic and safe under degraded conditions.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "LilacCreek", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:13:00.788412Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-03T22:28:56.360503Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-03T22:28:56.360435Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["connectors", "fcp-integration", "host-runtime"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.5.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.1.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:01.357088Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.5.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.2.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:17:01.859615Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "ft-3681t.5.1", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.5", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T19:13:00.788412Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1768, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.5.1", "author": "Jeff Emanuel", "text": "Implemented connector host runtime slice (lifecycle/protocol envelope/budget guardrails) with unit tests and deterministic e2e harness; e2e pass artifact at tests/e2e/logs/ft_3681t_5_1_20260303_000544.jsonl. Validation attempts run via rch exec: cargo check/clippy/fmt currently fail on pre-existing workspace issues outside this bead (frankenterm-gui unresolved k9/benchmarking, frankenterm-core bench WaModel/WaMsg gating, blob-leases clippy errors, broad fmt drift). Keeping bead in_progress pending policy on global blockers vs slice-local close.", "created_at": "2026-03-03T05:32:44Z"}, {"id": 1775, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.5.1", "author": "Jeff Emanuel", "text": "Added integration test coverage for connector host runtime degraded/recovery contracts in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/connector_host_runtime_integration.rs and extended tests/e2e/test_ft_3681t_5_1_connector_host_runtime.sh with integration-phase logging + FT_SKIP_LIB_TESTS/FT_CARGO_TARGET_DIR controls for deterministic artifacting under multi-agent contention. Validation: rch exec with isolated /tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR passed integration tests once (2 passed). Subsequent reruns are currently blocked by unrelated in-flight compile break in crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs (missing LaunchOutcome fields bootstrap_dispatches/pre_launch_checkpoint) from concurrent work.", "created_at": "2026-03-03T06:06:34Z"}, {"id": 1823, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.5.1", "author": "SapphireHarbor", "text": "Verification: connector_host_runtime 9/9 tests pass. cargo check --lib passes clean. Integration tests in tests/connector_host_runtime_integration.rs also compile and pass.", "created_at": "2026-03-03T22:28:52Z"}]}
 {"id": "ft-3681t.5.10", "title": "[connectors] Connector SDK/devkit and certification pipeline", "description": "Provide a robust connector developer kit: scaffold tooling, local harness, contract validation, policy linting, signed packaging workflow, and certification gates before registry publication.", "acceptance_criteria": "1. SDK scaffolding and local harness reduce connector onboarding friction without weakening security controls. 2. Certification pipeline enforces protocol, policy, and sandbox compliance before publish. 3. Developer docs and examples support reproducible connector authoring. 4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e tests with deterministic fixtures and detailed structured logs/artifacts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5.  {"id": "ft-3681t.7.3.2", "title": "[ft-3681t.7.3][support] Enforce rch for heavy cargo SendText policy path", "description": "Implement a bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3: add reusable heavy-cargo/rch classification helpers in build_coord and enforce require-approval for non-human SendText attempts that invoke heavy cargo commands without an rch wrapper. Keep scope to policy/build_coord/tests only.", "status": "in_progress", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "WhiteFox", "created_at": "2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-09T01:56:33.245598Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "ft-3681t.7.3.2", "depends_on_id": "ft-3681t.7.3", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 2036, "issue_id": "ft-3681t.7.3.2", "author": "WhiteFox", "text": "Implemented the bounded policy/build_coord slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs`: added `command_uses_rch`, `is_heavy_cargo_command`, `requires_rch_offload`, and `recommended_rch_prefix()`; the parser now handles env-prefixed commands and `rch exec -- cargo ...` wrappers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`: added `policy.rch.heavy_compute` so non-human `SendText` requests invoking heavy cargo without `rch` now return `RequireApproval` with an `rch` prefix recommendation.
- Added focused unit tests covering wrapped/unwrapped heavy cargo detection and policy decisions.

Validation:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` -> remote success on `vmi1149989`.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` in 2m07s (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Initial remote compile exposed an implementation bug (`shell_words::split` unresolved in `build_coord.rs`); fixed by switching to existing `shlex::split`, then reran the remote check successfully.

Remaining blocker:
- Targeted remote `cargo test` commands for the new unit tests are currently blocked by worker topology drift: scheduler repeatedly sends them to `vmi1227854`, which exits 101 because `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml` is missing on that worker. Implementation is complete; remote test execution is infra-blocked rather than code-blocked.
", "created_at": "2026-03-09T01:56:33Z"}]}
